pattern: The minimum of the array determines the floor: if the global minimum appears more than once, all copies survive (paired together they produce 0, but 0 disables further reduction); otherwise a single minimum can eliminate every larger element via modulo.
hints: Taking nums[i] % nums[j] when nums[j] divides nums[i] produces 0; zeros can only be eliminated in pairs, so each pair of zeros reduces count by one.
If the array minimum appears at least twice, every other element can be reduced to 0 using the minimum, but pairs of the same minimum give 0 too, so count the minimum's occurrences carefully.

If any element is strictly smaller than all others and not a divisor issue: a single minimum can absorb all larger elements one-by-one leaving just one element; if the minimum appears multiple times, each extra copy contributes to the final count via pairing.

edge_inputs.description: all elements equal, pairs reduce to zeros then pairs of zeros reduce
two elements, one larger than other
all distinct, minimum can absorb rest
two copies of minimum, rest eliminated
minimum appears many times, count matters
minimum divides everything but appears once

stress.description: random array with repeated small values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(size, 100000)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
